title: Who decides whether stabilized rents are raised

on_screen_text: There are over one million rent stabilized

Have you ever wondered how these rates
living in New York City.
Fair... it's not a game
It's how rents get decided for more than 2 million New Yorkers
They're an independent body that considers the evidence

This week, the RGB will start hearing from
I appointed six members
a range of experts dig into the data
frames.setting: Mixed setting: Mayor speaking at a podium in an ornate government room with US and NYC flags; intercut with close-up shots of Monopoly-style board game props labeled 'TENANTS' and 'LANDLORDS'
frames.presentation_style: Stylized explainer video mixing government office footage with playful Monopoly board game props as visual metaphor for rent policy

on_screen_text: If you're a tenant it's important the RGB hear from you

on how a rent hike or lack thereof would affect you.

frames.setting: Continuation of ornate government room with game props; also shows speaker at a desk surrounded by Monopoly miniature buildings and game board
visual_elements: Monopoly board game spread on desk
frames.presentation_style: Direct-to-camera with Monopoly board as prop; playful visual metaphor maintained throughout

on_screen_text: You probably know how I feel about what should happen
to the rent, but this is a chance to have your voices heard
by the people who make the final decision.
Your turn New York City.
